skill.name: langgraph-docs
skill.description: Fetches the LangGraph documentation index and selected docs so assistants can answer LangGraph Python questions with source-grounded guidance.
skill.summary: Retrieves relevant LangGraph documentation for Python implementation help.
skill.author: langchain-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one hardcoded URL and four weak cryptography matches. The URL is an intentional LangChain documentation index, and the weak cryptography alerts are false positives from prose rather than cryptographic code. No prompt injection, command execution, filesystem access, or secret exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Fetch
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to fetch the LangChain documentation index at https://docs.langchain.com/llms.txt. This is expected for a documentation helper, but it requires network access and may disclose the general documentation request to that service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is directly present in the skill instructions. The risk is low because the destination is the official documentation index and no secrets or user data are sent by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in documentation prose and selection guidance, not in executable cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption API, or weak algorithm use is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence that this is a real security issue is very low. The file contains only Markdown instructions and has no cryptographic implementation or executable code path.

content.value_statement: LangGraph questions often need current documentation instead of memory-based answers. This skill directs Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to fetch the LangGraph docs index and read the most relevant pages before answering.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the LangChain documentation index from docs.langchain.com.
Selects relevant LangGraph pages based on the user's question.
Prioritizes how-to guides, concepts, tutorials, and reference pages.
Reads selected documentation pages before giving implementation guidance.
Supports source-grounded answers for LangGraph Python topics.
limitations: Requires network access to reach the LangChain documentation site.
Depends on the documentation index being available and accurate.
Does not execute, test, or validate LangGraph code.
Focuses on LangGraph documentation, not broad LangChain support.

use_cases.title: Implement a LangGraph Feature
use_cases.description: Find the right how-to pages before building graph state, nodes, edges, or checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Explain LangGraph Concepts
use_cases.description: Use concept pages to explain graphs, state management, persistence, and agent workflows.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Architecture
use_cases.description: Reference official documentation while reviewing a planned LangGraph design or migration.

prompt_templates.title: Answer a Basic Question
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a simple explanation grounded in current docs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the LangGraph docs to explain how state works in LangGraph Python. Keep the answer concise.

prompt_templates.title: Find an Implementation Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when building a specific LangGraph feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the relevant LangGraph docs for checkpointing in Python, then summarize the recommended implementation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Design Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when choosing between documented approaches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the LangGraph documentation to compare persistence options for a multi-step agent workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Ground a Code Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when reviewing an existing LangGraph implementation against documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the relevant LangGraph Python docs and review this graph design for missing concepts, incorrect assumptions, and documentation-backed improvements.

output_examples.input: How should I add persistence to a LangGraph Python workflow?
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies checkpointing documentation, summarizes the required concepts, and explains the implementation steps in plain language.

output_examples.input: What docs should I read before building a conditional graph?
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the most relevant concept and how-to pages, then explains why each page matters for conditional routing.

output_examples.input: Review my LangGraph architecture plan against the docs.
output_examples.output: The assistant maps the plan to documented LangGraph concepts and highlights likely gaps before implementation.

best_practices: Ask for the specific LangGraph task, concept, or error you need to resolve.
Request citations or page names when documentation traceability matters.
Use the fetched guidance as a starting point, then test code in your own environment.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for unrelated Python libraries or general web research.
Do not assume fetched documentation replaces testing or version checks.
Do not provide secrets, private URLs, or proprietary code unless necessary.

faq.question: Does this skill install LangGraph?
faq.answer: No. It only guides the assistant to read LangGraph documentation and answer questions.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs network access to fetch the LangChain documentation index and selected pages.

faq.question: Can it answer API reference questions?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions tell the assistant to use reference docs when API details are needed.

faq.question: Can it validate my code?
faq.answer: It can compare code against documentation, but it does not run tests or execute the code.

faq.question: Is it limited to Python?
faq.answer: The skill description focuses on LangGraph Python documentation.

faq.question: Why does it fetch an index first?
faq.answer: The index helps the assistant choose the most relevant documentation pages before answering.
